response_text:
# The Case for Dedicated Bus Lanes: A Solution Hiding in Plain Sight

Every morning, thousands of commuters sit in identical gridlock, watching the clock tick away while a nearly empty bus crawls past in the same stalled traffic. This is the paradox of modern urban transportation: we have a proven solution to congestion sitting right in front of us, and we're too stubborn to use it. Dedicated bus lanes aren't a radical experiment—they're a pragmatic, evidence-backed approach to moving more people through congested corridors. It's time we stopped treating traffic as an unsolvable problem and started treating buses as the mass transit solution they could be.

The case begins with a simple reality check. Our roads are reaching a breaking point. The average commuter in major urban areas loses hundreds of hours each year sitting in traffic. The economic cost runs into the billions—delayed deliveries, lost productivity, wasted fuel
